title: Connect from podman container to host service
body:
### Feature request description

I need to connect to a service (e.g. REST API) on my windows workstation.
This is where podman desktop is running.

podman-engine is running inside WSL2 (Ubuntu).
I tried a lot of combinations of service hosting on ANY ip address instead of only 127.0.0.1 on the host combined with calls to `host.containers.internal` from inside the container. Connection refused. I also tried --net="host" but to no avail (on a side note: networking-tools:extra failed to stat because nginx tried to bind to port 80 - operation not permitted).

If Windows firewall is part of this problem I can instantly shoot myself as it is company managed. Security first, developer experience second. Is there something I need to do which Docker for windows maybe did in the background? Network? Routing?

```
bash-5.1# ip route
default via 10.0.2.2 dev tap0
10.0.2.0/24 dev tap0 proto kernel scope link src 10.0.2.100
```

I have no idea where this 10.0.2.0/24 network is coming from. It's not part of my WSL.

### Suggest potential solution

Documentation or FAQ entry how to make this work.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: is the host service listening on 10.0.2.1?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, closing.
